summary: Implementation matches all spec requirements. EMIT_REFCOUNT_CALLS=true is set in codegen/cranelift/mod.rs. All 22 borrowed-reference runtime functions have retain_if_ptr calls in rc.rs. GcState.enabled=true. The implementation diff contains 13 #[test] functions covering retain_if_ptr, list/dict/tuple/getattr/global/cell/closure owned references, emit_refcount_enabled, gc_enabled, and conformance tests. All test plan items from the spec are represented. Release-before-overwrite, Copy retain, and return cleanup all activate with the flag. Driver per-session state reset is in place.
findings: HARD PASS: Code matches all spec requirements — R1 (ownership audit table in rc.rs), R2 (retain_if_ptr on 22 borrowed-reference functions), R3 (EMIT_REFCOUNT_CALLS=true), R4 (GcState.enabled=true), R6 (conformance test un-ignored)
HARD PASS: Spec has Test Plan section AND implementation diff contains 13 #[test] functions covering all listed test cases
HARD PASS: Existing tests still pass — no regressions introduced
SOFT PASS: Code quality — conditional blocks guarded by EMIT_REFCOUNT_CALLS follow consistent pattern across mod.rs and jit.rs
SOFT PASS: R5 (ASan validation) is a manual validation step, not automatable in the diff — this is expected
